---
title: "Code Quality"
description: "Make code trustworthy with tests, style, formatting, linting, types, and automation."
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [code-quality]
order: 360
---

## Why this chapter matters

Make code trustworthy with tests, style, formatting, linting, types, and automation.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.callout-note title="Guiding questions"}
- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Testing with pytest](testing-with-pytest.qmd) — Use assertions, pytest, and edge cases to check behavior.
- [Style, Formatting, and Linting](style-formatting-and-linting.qmd) — Use PEP 8 and Ruff to keep Python readable and consistent.
- [Type Checking](type-checking.qmd) — Use type hints and a checker to catch mismatched value types early.
- [Pre-commit and CI](pre-commit-and-ci.qmd) — Run checks automatically before commits and on shared repositories.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "code-quality-overview",
  "title": "Code Quality overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Code Quality chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Make code trustworthy with tests, style, formatting, linting, types, and automation.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Make code trustworthy with tests, style, formatting, linting, types, and automation."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
